what_it_does: This file is a ledger/model-card entry that records the download status, metadata, intended use cases, known quirks, open questions, and registration notes for a specific GGUF-quantized variant of the Gemma-4-26B-A4B-it model.
observations: The model is downloaded but not yet registered or served through Ollama.
HF id is unsloth/gemma-4-26B-A4B-it-GGUF, file is ~12 GB, Q3_K_M quantization.
Architecture is Gemma 4 MoE with 4B active parameters per token; upstream family supports omnimodal input but GGUF retention is unverified.
It was pulled as a non-Qwen alternative for the chat slot to diversify lineage and behavior.
A full-precision Gemma-4-E4B-FP8 sibling was tested earlier and removed; this GGUF is a larger model at lower bit depth.
Suggested fits include single-stream high-quality reasoning and potentially multimodal text+audio.
No actual tested performance data is present; the section lists questions to characterize.
Registration command is provided: ollama create gemma-4-26b-a4b-q3km -f Modelfile with FROM pointing to the GGUF path.
Quirks include A4B compute cost between Qwen 3B-active MoE and dense models, uncertain modality retention, and uncertain chat-template alignment.
Open items include verifying modality retention, deciding whether to register/characterize/remove, comparing quality to Qwen 27B at the same bit depth, and testing tool calling.
Cross-links point to the models hub, competing Qwen models, and the Ollama operations note.
standouts: The entry is prospective inventory: downloaded but not yet served, so most claims are to-be-verified.
It explicitly frames the model as a non-Qwen lineage alternative, making lineage diversification a first-class rationale.
The central uncertainty is whether the GGUF retains Gemma 4's omnimodal capabilities or is effectively text-only.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with type, id, filename, status, runtime, date_added, and tags.
Uses wiki-style cross-references to hub pages, related models, and operations notes.
Divides content into structured sections: at-a-glance table, source, fits, tested performance, launch config, quirks, open items, and connects-to.
Uses Ollama-specific vocabulary: GGUF, Modelfile, mmproj, ollama create/show.
concerns: Several claims are speculative or unverified (multimodal retention, tool calling, chat-template alignment, throughput) and are flagged as open questions, but fact and hypothesis still sit in the same sections.
The 'Fits these needs' section links to multimodal text+audio even though modality retention is explicitly unverified, creating a slight purpose/status mismatch.
The 'Tested performance' heading contains no tested data, only a list of future characterization tasks.
The date 2026-05-27 is treated as a ledger timestamp without context on how it relates to present time.
notes: Inventory card for a downloaded-but-unserved Gemma-4 MoE GGUF, documenting why it was pulled, how to register it, and what still needs verification before it can be trusted as a chat backend.
questions: What is the exact local filesystem path or symlink for the 12 GB GGUF file?
Does the unsloth repo for this specific variant ship an mmproj-F16.gguf file for multimodal support?
What are Tim's typical queries and evaluation criteria for the planned comparison against Qwen models?
What hardware constraints or concurrency targets drive the proposed 4080 load test?
Is a complete Modelfile already prepared, or is only the FROM line defined?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is to record and plan evaluation of a downloaded model alternative; the file does that, though it reads more like a to-do/verification checklist than a validated runtime record.
apparent_themes: Model inventory as a living ledger with explicit uncertainty.
Lineage diversification to reduce dependency on a single model family.
Quantization trade-offs among size, active compute, capability, and modality retention.
Ollama-centric local model operations and registration workflow.
intention_signals: The entry is trying to turn a downloaded artifact into an actionable evaluation candidate by surfacing every assumption that must be checked before it can replace or complement the incumbent Qwen models.
novelty: It treats a model file as a code/design substrate in a ledger, mixing operational status (downloaded/not-yet-served), lineage rationale (non-Qwen alternative), and modality-retention risk in one card rather than a conventional static model card.
contribution: It preserves the rationale for selecting this specific GGUF variant, the exact registration command, the open verification checklist, and the cross-links to related models and Ollama operations so a fused system can reason about candidate models without re-deriving why it was pulled.
summary_for_embedding: A ledger model card for the downloaded 12 GB Gemma-4-26B-A4B-it GGUF Q3_K_M variant, documenting its status, intended use as a non-Qwen chat alternative, Ollama registration steps, and unverified multimodal retention.
intention_for_embedding: To track a candidate local language model through download, registration, and characterization before deciding whether to keep or remove it.
